steps.title: Identify the defining clue
steps.content: Esteemed and characteristic works are positive descriptors of Edwards's portrait work. These signal we need a phrase that expresses something positive and defining about this body of work.

steps.title: Choose the phrase that fits
steps.content:
The blank fills "British painter Peter Edwards has a ______ painting portraits" — the next sentence calls his portraits "esteemed" and "characteristic works," signaling positive recognition. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — reputation for: being widely known and recognized for something — fits; Edwards is known for painting notable portraits, and the esteemed examples confirm this positive standing. ✔
  • Choice B — requirement for: a need or obligation — this says Edwards is required to paint portraits, implying obligation rather than recognition; nothing in the passage suggests compulsion. ✘
  • Choice C — complaint about: an expression of dissatisfaction — this says Edwards is known for objecting to portrait painting, which contradicts the positive framing of "esteemed" works. ✘
  • Choice D — question about: an expression of doubt — this says Edwards has doubts about portrait painting, again contradicting the positive context of characteristic, esteemed works. ✘
That confirms A is correct.
